items.title: Panthers Bolster Offensive Line with No. 19 Pick Monroe Freeling
items.summary: The Carolina Panthers added a towering presence to their offensive front Thursday night, selecting offensive tackle Monroe Freeling with the 19th overall pick in the NFL Draft.  Freeling, a 6-foot-7 lineman out of Georgia Bulldogs football, arrives in Carolina with the kind of size and pedigree teams covet when investing in protection up front.  A native of Mount Pleasant, South Carolina, Freeling started 13 games last season for Georgia and earned second-team All-SEC honors in 2025, solidifying his status as one of the top offensive tackles in this year’s class.

items.title: Vikings use first-round pick on Florida DT Caleb Banks, who broke his foot before the combine
items.summary: The Minnesota Vikings selected Florida defensive tackle Caleb Banks with the 18th pick in the first round of the NFL draft on Thursday night, adding some interior bulk and athleticism with a raw prospect whose recent injury likely pushed him down the board.  The 6-foot-6, 327-pound Banks cracked the fourth metatarsal bone in his left foot in February while practicing for the 40-yard dash the night before the NFL scouting combine in Indianapolis.  The Vikings signed veteran defensive tackles Jonathan Allen and Javon Hargrave in free agency last year but cut them both for salary cap savings last month.
